qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded file size permits denial-of-service through resource exhaustion
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding concerns unbounded tar file-size parsing and resource exhaustion, not the advisory's drive-relative symlink linkpath stripping and path traversal overwrite root cause.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described node-tar root cause: drive-relative symlink targets are validated before stripping and then used to create an escaping symlink that enables overwrite outside the extraction cwd.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-9ppj-qmqm-q256 describes `tar` extraction of a symlink with `linkpath: "C:../../../target.txt"`, where `Unpack[STRIPABSOLUTEPATH]` validates the pre-stripped value and later creates a symlink to `../../../target.txt`.

The only manifest-listed qualifying report, `unbounded_file_size_dos`, cites `src/parse.ts`, `src/header.ts`, `src/read-entry.ts`, and `src/large-numbers.ts` for unbounded file-size handling and denial of service, not symlink linkpath stripping or outside-root overwrite.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding exists, so Tier1, Tier2, and Tier3 do not apply.
